title: Repo attribution fails for hyphenated repo names (dolt_database underscore-sanitizes the id prefix)
description:
Discovered via slice-9 real-machine smoke test. refresh::read_prefix reads .beads/metadata.json dolt_database as the id prefix, but bd stores dolt_database with hyphens replaced by underscores (Dolt DB names disallow hyphens), while issue IDs keep hyphens.

Evidence (real repos):
- reading-lite: dolt_database=reading_lite, IDs=reading-lite-hck.1 -> attributed [unknown]
- federated-beads: dolt_database=federated_beads, IDs=federated-beads-dxh -> [unknown]
- agent-skills: dolt_database=as, IDs=as-zvu -> OK (prefix has no hyphen)
- approach/megaclock: prefix == dolt_database, no hyphen -> OK

Likely fix: in refresh::read_prefix (or PrefixMap matching), map underscores back to hyphens (id_prefix = dolt_database.replace('_','-')), guarded by a test with a hyphenated fixture and the risk that a prefix could contain a real underscore. Belongs to the refresh/attribution layer (slice 4), not slice 9 rendering; slice 9's view faithfully renders whatever attribution it is given, including the unknown bucket.

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text:
Real-machine verification (cargo run -- snapshot, 7-repo config).
BEFORE (buggy): [unknown]=21 — contained every federated-beads-*, reading-lite-*, session-tui-* id. Buckets: approach 72, megaclock 25, unknown 21, agent-skills 13.
AFTER (fixed):  [unknown]=0. Buckets: approach 72, megaclock 25, agent-skills 13, federated-beads 9, session-tui 7, reading-lite 4.
Every hyphenated-prefix repo now attributes to its own bucket. (The ~/dev/beads roster entry still logs a genuine 'no beads project found' export warning — unrelated to this bug.)

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text:
Autoreview (Codex gpt-5.6-sol, high) — 5 rounds:
R1 P2: lossy dolt_database match not unique (a_b-c vs a-b_c). 
R2 P1: rejected frequency-based disambiguation (no majority invariant for a federation tool). 
R3 P2: rsplit_once('-') misses partitioned ids (federated-beads-mol-1o4). 
R4 P2: a lossy match still can't prove ownership.
Root cause across all four: reconstructing the prefix from metadata/ids is fundamentally lossy. Pivoted to bd's authoritative effective prefix via 'bd config get issue_prefix --json' (BdClient::issue_prefix). 
R5: CLEAN — 'patch is correct' (0.95). No findings skipped.

title: fbd v1: Federated Beads read-only TUI over bd hub
description: Implement fbd per the reviewed TDD plan in plans/fbd-v1-implementation-plan.md. Rust + ratatui read-only TUI federating multiple beads repos via a bd-managed hub workspace (bd repo add/sync hydration). v1 = cross-repo ready list (grouped/filterable), detail pane, cross-repo search, copy-context action, TUI-owned async refresh with staleness indicator. Non-goals: writes, blocked view, daemon, bd --global. All bd access via BdClient trait; unit tests on recorded --json fixtures; gated integration suite builds real fixture repos with bd init in tempdirs.
acceptance_criteria: cargo fmt --check, clippy -D warnings, cargo test green without bd; gated integration suite green with bd; fbd repos discover ~/dev finds the 5 real repos; fbd shows merged ready list <3s; per-repo failure degrades to status warning; fbd reset rebuilds hub
close_reason: fbd v1 complete: all 13 slices + P1 attribution fix merged to local main. 206 unit + 7 integration tests, all gates green, acceptance checklist 4/4. Remaining open children (dxh.14-16,18-19,21, 23v, yvp) are v1.x/v2 backlog refinements, intentionally left open.

title: TUI polish: detail pane border/padding, j/k navigates beads in detail, hide beads repo
description: Three UI polish items: (1) faint border between list and detail panes plus left padding for the detail pane; (2) with the detail pane open, j/k move through the bead list (refetching the detail) instead of scrolling the pane — J/K take over pane scrolling; (3) rows attributed to the beads tool's own repo are excluded from the ready list and search results.

title: Hub lock protocol should cover ensure_hub and reset, not just refresh::run
description:
Autoreview (slice 9, round 4) noted that hub reconciliation runs outside the advisory hub lock: gather_snapshot / run_snapshot call hub::ensure_hub (init + repo_add + config.yaml rewrite) BEFORE refresh::run acquires <hub>/.fbd.lock. Two fbd instances starting together can race hub init / repo_add / config.yaml; hub::reset can race the same section too.

Pre-existing, not introduced by slice 9: run_snapshot (slice 6) uses the same ensure_hub -> refresh::run ordering, and ensure_hub's own doc comment explicitly defers cross-process safety to the slice-4 lock (the lock was scoped to refresh::run). Fix belongs at the hub/refresh locking layer, not in slice 9's render+runtime.

Proposed: extend the lock to cover ensure_hub through refresh (acquire before ensure_hub, hold across the sync), and make reset participate in the same protocol. Add a concurrency test (two ensure_hub racing).

title: fbd-slice
description: Per-slice implementation workflow for the federated-beads epic: plan, TDD implementation, autoreview, local merge.

title: Slice 12: Copy-context action + release polish + acceptance checklist
description: Red (unit): builds_cd_command (cd <repo> && bd show <id>), builds_markdown_block, unattributed_issue_falls_back_to_hub_show; y=cd command, Y=markdown. Green: OSC 52 clipboard writer (works over ssh/tmux, zero deps), status-bar confirmation. Polish: --version/--help review; README install/quickstart/keybindings/architecture/v2 notes. Manual acceptance checklist recorded in PR: fresh-machine simulation via discover, dead repo path degrades to warning, fbd reset rebuilds hub, cargo test green with bd renamed away (integration prints SKIP). See plans/fbd-v1-implementation-plan.md, Slice 12, for full red/green/refactor detail.
acceptance_criteria: Context-builder tests green; y/Y copy via OSC52 with confirmation; all four acceptance checklist items pass and are recorded in the PR

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text:
Acceptance checklist (run for real; user's live 7-repo config backed up and RESTORED intact each time):
1. Fresh-machine sim: moved real config+data aside; 'fbd repos discover /Users/brian/dev --add' found 7 repos; 'fbd snapshot' showed 129 merged attributed rows (e.g. [federated-beads] P1 federated-beads-dxh, [approach] P1 approach-0lw.11). PASS.
2. Dead repo path: scratch config with a nonexistent path -> 'fbd snapshot' exited 0 with 'warning: roster path does not exist: ...', empty list, no hard failure. PASS.
3. fbd reset -> rebuild: post-reset snapshot 129 rows == pre-reset 129, content identical (diff clean). PASS.
4. cargo test with bd off PATH: all 7 integration tests print SKIP and pass; with bd present: 206 unit + 7 integration green. PASS.
Real config/data restored and verified (doctor shows the original 7 repos).

title: Slice 10: Detail pane
description: Red (unit): enter_requests_detail (reduce emits Effect::FetchDetail), detail_arrives_and_renders, detail_fetch_error_shows_message, esc_returns_to_list. Red (render): renders_detail_pane with title, wrapped description, blocker dependency line with status. Green: split layout (right pane >=100 cols else stacked), lazy fetch on Enter only, executed on worker thread via bd show <id> --json against hub. Refactor: effect-executor table in runtime for reuse by search. See plans/fbd-v1-implementation-plan.md, Slice 10, for full red/green/refactor detail.
acceptance_criteria: All detail tests green; cursor movement causes zero bd calls; Enter fetches exactly once

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text:
Autoreview (codex gpt-5.6-sol, high; branch vs main) — 3 findings:
1. Same-id re-open race (P2): DetailReady matches only by id, so X→Esc→X can let an older worker's response overwrite the newer pane. FILED as federated-beads-dxh.20 (P3) — low severity, same-issue data, beyond the plan's different-id stale-drop scope.
2. Hidden list navigation while detail open (P2): j/k/f/p mutated the underlying selection in Detail mode, so Esc could return to a different row. FIXED — reduce now gates SelectNext/SelectPrev/CycleRepoFilter/TogglePriorityFilter to ViewMode::List; regression test navigation_inert_while_detail_open.
3. Missing labels (P2): master plan Slice 10 lists labels; bd show emits a labels array when present, but Issue had no labels field so they were discarded. FIXED — added Issue.labels (serde default), rendered in the detail meta; tests parses_labels_and_defaults_when_absent + renders_detail_labels.

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text: Autoreview converged clean after 4 rounds (codex gpt-5.6-sol, high; branch vs main). Round 2 also folded the same-id race (dxh.20) into a token-based fix rather than deferring; round 3 fixed selection identity across refresh while the pane is open. Final: 153 unit + 6 integration tests green; fmt/clippy clean.

title: Slice 11: Cross-repo search
description: Red (unit): slash_opens_search_input, typing_edits_query, enter_submits_search_effect, results_replace_rows_with_attribution (same PrefixMap path as ready), esc_restores_ready_list. Red (render): renders_search_input_and_result_count. Green: Effect::Search(query) -> bd search <q> --json on hub; results reuse row rendering; mode flag in App. Refactor: unify row-list state so ready/search share selection + filter code. See plans/fbd-v1-implementation-plan.md, Slice 11, for full red/green/refactor detail.
acceptance_criteria: All search tests green; search results carry repo attribution; Esc restores prior ready list and selection

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text: Autoreview (codex gpt-5.6-sol, high; branch vs main) converged over 8 rounds; all 7 findings accepted and fixed, none rejected or deferred: (1) P2 pasted-key decode race -> decode raw keys on the UI thread; (2) P2 search-opened detail moved the ready selection on refresh -> relocate only when search.is_none(); (3) P3 static search hint + double error prefix; (4) P3 loading/error advertised inert keys; (5) P2 / search undiscoverable in LIST_HINTS; (6) P2 stale results shown under a new query -> render rows only in Results; (7) P2 filter-hidden results were a blank pane -> NO_MATCH_HINT + f/p hint. Round 8 clean. Filed federated-beads-dxh.21 (v2: cache the refresh PrefixMap instead of rebuilding per search).

title: Slice 9: Ready view rendering + terminal runtime (TUI launches)
description: Red (render, TestBackend 80x24): renders_group_headers_and_rows, renders_selection_highlight, renders_status_bar_with_age_and_warnings (injected now for deterministic age), renders_empty_state_hint. Red (unit): runtime refresh task sends Started then Done via mpsc with FakeBdClient. Green: view::draw(frame,&app,now); runtime.rs event loop (crossterm event thread + refresh worker -> one mpsc channel), terminal setup/teardown with panic hook; bare fbd launches TUI: ensure_hub warnings to status, initial refresh spawned. Manual smoke documented: real 5 repos, list <3s, r keeps UI responsive. See plans/fbd-v1-implementation-plan.md, Slice 9, for full red/green/refactor detail.
acceptance_criteria: Render + runtime tests green; fbd shows grouped ready list against real repos; refresh is async with stale rows still visible

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text:
Slice 9 manual smoke (real machine, /Users/brian/dev):

DISCOVER: 'fbd repos discover /Users/brian/dev --add' found and added 7 real beads repos (agent-skills, approach, beads, federated-beads, megaclock, reading-lite, session-tui). No prior config existed.

SNAPSHOT: 'fbd snapshot' returned 59 ready rows across the repos with correct sorting (P0/P1 first) and repo attribution for single-token prefixes. Warm run ~4.9s wall for 7 repos (6 valid), well under the 10s stop threshold; cold run (with export+sync) ~9.4s incl. cargo overhead. Real data flows end-to-end into the read model the TUI view renders.

GRACEFUL DEGRADATION verified: the 'beads' repo is not a bd project at that path -> per-repo export + metadata warnings on stderr, other repos still hydrated and printed. This is exactly the non-fatal degrade path the runtime's gather_snapshot uses for the status bar.

ATTRIBUTION BUG (filed federated-beads-dxh.17, P1): hyphenated multi-word repos (reading-lite, federated-beads) show as [unknown] because metadata.json dolt_database underscore-sanitizes the id prefix (reading_lite) while IDs keep hyphens (reading-lite-hck). Slice-4/attribution concern, out of slice-9 scope; the slice-9 view faithfully renders the unknown bucket.

INTERACTIVE TUI: not run by the agent (no tty in the harness). Bare 'fbd' launches the TUI (main.rs -> runtime::run); interactive launch + 'r' responsiveness smoke is PENDING THE USER.

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text:
Autoreview round 4 (codex gpt-5.6-sol, high) — 2 findings:
- [P1] detached refresh workers on shutdown: FIXED. event_loop now tracks refresh-worker join handles and joins any in-flight worker on quit, so a quit mid-refresh waits for the bd subprocess to finish and release the hub lock (no orphaned child mutating the hub after fbd's lock drops). Finished handles are pruned on each spawn.
- [P2] ensure_hub runs outside the hub lock: SKIPPED (out of scope) -> filed federated-beads-dxh.19. Pre-existing: run_snapshot (slice 6) does the same ensure_hub->refresh ordering and ensure_hub's doc explicitly defers cross-process safety to the slice-4 lock. Reworking the lock protocol across hub.rs/refresh.rs is a separate concern, not slice 9's render+runtime scope.

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text: Autoreview converged (codex gpt-5.6-sol, high; branch vs main): round 6 CLEAN — 'no accepted/actionable findings', patch correct (0.9). Six rounds total; findings fixed: viewport scroll, loading-forever, setup rollback, tick redraw, input-thread hang (r1-2); flat-order nav, declined-refresh None, filter-vs-empty hint, best-effort restore (r3-4... );  real diagnostics in status bar (r3); shutdown worker join + unconditional cleanup, sticky header, drop inert key hint (r4-5). Skipped/filed: dxh.17 (attribution), dxh.18 (v2 grouped-nav), dxh.19 (lock protocol scope).

title: Slice 7: Roster CLI — fbd repos add/remove/list/discover
description: Red (unit): add_appends_and_dedupes, remove_by_path, list_prints_roster, discover_finds_beads_dirs (scans <root>/*/.beads one level deep), discover_skips_already_added, add_rejects_dir_without_beads (instructive error). Green: subcommands mutate Config via config.rs. Refactor: canonicalize paths on store, explicit ~ expansion. See plans/fbd-v1-implementation-plan.md, Slice 7, for full red/green/refactor detail.
acceptance_criteria: All six unit tests green; discover on a fixture tree finds exactly the dirs containing .beads

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text: Slice 7 autoreview (codex gpt-5.6-sol): round 1 accepted+fixed two path-normalization defects (stale-repo removal via parent-canonicalize fallback; add/discover dedupe by normalizing stored entries) with regression tests. Round 2 consciously rejected: (a) roster read-modify-write concurrency race — speculative for a human-driven personal roster CLI, Config::save already atomic; filed federated-beads-yvp. (b) remove by since-dangling symlink alias — unresolvable by path once the link is gone; documented the limitation in store_path/remove docstrings and pointed at removing via the canonical path from 'fbd repos list'.

title: Slice 8: App state machine (pure reduce, no I/O)
description: Red (unit): starts_in_loading_then_shows_rows, selection_moves_and_clamps, repo_filter_cycles, priority_filter_toggles, refresh_while_stale_keeps_rows, refresh_error_surfaces_in_status, quit_msg_sets_done. Green: App{rows,filtered_ix,selection,filter,status,view_mode}, Msg enum (keys + refresh lifecycle), pure reduce(state,msg)->state; keys.rs maps crossterm KeyEvent->Msg with its own tests (q,/,r,y,Enter,Esc). Refactor: FilterSet; invariant selection within filtered bounds. See plans/fbd-v1-implementation-plan.md, Slice 8, for full red/green/refactor detail.
acceptance_criteria: All reducer + keymap tests green; reduce has no I/O and no clock access (now injected)

title: Slice 6: Headless tracer bullet — fbd snapshot, version gate, doctor, reset CLI
description: End-to-end tracer bullet before any TUI. Red (unit): run_snapshot(cfg,bd,out) prints '[repo] P1 <id> <title>' lines and --json emits Snapshot; version-gate predicate tests (>=1.1.0 and schema_version==1) against fixture + doctored versions; doctor lists missing repo as MISSING. Red (integration): snapshot_command_end_to_end against real fixture repos with correct attribution. Green: clap CLI with snapshot/reset/doctor subcommands wired ensure_hub->refresh->fetch->print; startup version gate with clear failure message. See plans/fbd-v1-implementation-plan.md, Slice 6, for full red/green/refactor detail.
acceptance_criteria: fbd snapshot works against real fixture repos; version gate blocks old/skewed bd with actionable message; doctor shows bd version, paths, roster health
close_reason: Slice 6 complete: headless snapshot/doctor/reset CLI + version gate, merged to main, autoreview converged

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text: Autoreview (codex gpt-5.6-sol, branch vs main) — 4 findings. ACCEPTED+fixed: (1) unconditional roster load broke 'fbd reset' and prevented doctor from diagnosing a bad config — main now loads roster per-command; reset needs none; doctor loads it itself and reports parse errors. (2) version gate accepted incomplete versions (1.1, 2) despite fail-closed contract — parse_version now requires all 3 numeric components. REJECTED as consciously-deferred design boundary (lock scoped to 'repo sync' on disposable hub, Slices 3/4): (a) lock ensure_hub reconciliation, (b) serialize reset against active hub ops. Filed follow-up federated-beads-dxh.16 under the epic.

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text: Autoreview converged (codex gpt-5.6-sol). Additional accepted+fixed across rounds beyond the first two: terminal-control-injection sweep (format_row, doctor roster output, snapshot warning loops all sanitize bd/config-derived fields; JSON untouched); load_roster uses symlink_metadata so an unreadable/dangling config errors instead of silently-empty; run_reset uses symlink_metadata so a removed dangling hub symlink reports 'removed'. Final remaining finding = the ensure_hub/reset lock-scope one, consciously rejected (design boundary; requires refactoring refresh::run's lock API across merged slices) and filed as federated-beads-dxh.16. See plans/slices/slice-6.md 'Autoreview outcomes'.

title: Slice 4: Refresh pipeline + prefix map + collision detection
description: Red (unit, FakeBdClient): exports_all_then_syncs_once, collects_per_repo_errors (one repo failing does not stop sync or other repos), builds_prefix_map from .beads/metadata.json dolt_database, flags_prefix_collisions, longest_prefix_wins (app vs app2). Red (integration): refresh_two_repos (prefixes ra, rb) asserts hub ready contains both and attribution is correct. Green: refresh::run(bd,roster,hub)->RefreshOutcome{prefix_map,errors,synced_at}; PrefixMap::repo_for(id) longest-prefix-then-dash match. Sequential exports (parallelism is v2). See plans/fbd-v1-implementation-plan.md, Slice 4, for full red/green/refactor detail.
acceptance_criteria: All five unit tests + integration test green; a failing repo yields RepoError while others hydrate

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text: Slice 4 requirement surfaced by Slice 3 autoreview: the planned process-level hub lock (<hub>/.fbd.lock) must be held across ensure_hub's init + roster read + repo_add reconciliation, not only repo sync. Two concurrent first-run fbd processes can otherwise both pass ensure_hub's is_initialized check (one aborts on bd's 'already initialized' exit-1) or both read a stale config.yaml and race repo_add / config rewrites. ensure_hub is deliberately single-process-only today (src/hub.rs).

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text:
Slice 4 autoreview (Codex gpt-5.6-sol, high): 3 findings, all fixed (none skipped). Second pass clean (0.91, patch correct).

P1 — export written to caller's cwd, not the source repo. bd resolves a relative `-o` against the PROCESS cwd, not the `-C` dir (verified vs bd 1.1.0); the Slice 2 argv_export comment was factually wrong. Every refresh export clobbered fbd's cwd and repo sync consumed stale source exports (smoking gun: this repo's .beads/issues.jsonl had been overwritten with rb fixture data). Fix: argv_export joins the output onto the repo path. refresh_two_repos strengthened with a post-ensure marker issue so refresh's own export is load-bearing.

P2 — repo_for let a shorter unique prefix mask a longer collided one. Collided prefixes now stay in the lookup table as None so they still win longest-match and resolve to None (ambiguous).

P2 — run now dedupes roster entries by canonical path (mirrors ensure_hub), so an aliased duplicate exports once and is not a false self-collision.

title: Slice 5: Snapshot read model
description: Red (unit, FakeBdClient + ready.json fixture): merges_ready_with_attribution, sorts_by_priority_then_updated (0 first, then updated_at desc), groups_by_repo, unattributed_goes_to_unknown_bucket. Green: snapshot::fetch(bd,hub,prefix_map)->Snapshot{rows:Vec<Row{issue,repo_name}>,fetched_at}, serializable for later --json output. UI-agnostic. See plans/fbd-v1-implementation-plan.md, Slice 5, for full red/green/refactor detail.
acceptance_criteria: All four unit tests green; Snapshot serializes to JSON

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text:
Autoreview (Codex gpt-5.6-sol, high) — 3 passes:
Pass 1: P2 duplicate directory basenames collapse distinct repos in grouping/filtering — FIXED (made repo_name roster-unique).
Pass 2: (a) P2 full filesystem paths leaked into serialized repo_name / violated basename contract — FIXED by disambiguating collided basenames with the unique id-prefix ('api (ra)' vs 'api (rb)'), added PrefixMap::attribution; (b) P2 lexical updated_at sort not universally chronological for RFC3339 — REJECTED as speculative for bd's contract: bd emits homogeneous whole-second UTC-Z timestamps within one ready call, so lexical == chronological, and the id-asc final tiebreak keeps ordering total/deterministic. Adding a datetime crate is a project-wide decision, deferred (see follow-up bead). Assumption documented inline at the comparator.
Pass 3: clean ('patch is correct', 0.93).

title: Slice 3: Hub lifecycle — ensure_hub + reset
description: Red (unit, FakeBdClient): creates_hub_when_missing, adds_missing_repos_only, tolerates_absent_repo_paths (warning not error). Red (integration): ensure_hub_end_to_end with two fixture repos asserts hub .beads/config.yaml lists both. Green: hub::ensure_hub(bd,paths,roster)->HubStatus{warnings}; read hub roster via bd repo list --json, with pre-specified fallback to parsing config.yaml repos.additional if --json unsupported (verify against real bd in red phase and record fixture). Also hub::reset deleting hub dir with path-containment assertion. Refactor: idempotency — double ensure_hub adds nothing twice. See plans/fbd-v1-implementation-plan.md, Slice 3, for full red/green/refactor detail.
acceptance_criteria: Unit + integration tests green; ensure_hub idempotent; reset refuses paths outside data dir
close_reason: Slice 3 complete: hub lifecycle (ensure_hub + reset) implemented, autoreviewed, merged to main

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text: Autoreview: fixed 3 single-process correctness findings — (1) within-run dedupe of duplicate/aliased roster entries via a mutable tracked set; (2) resolve hub-relative stored config.yaml entries against the hub dir before canonicalize; (3) genuine bd init failures propagate. DEFERRED (out of scope, recorded): full cross-process concurrency safety of ensure_hub — the init check-then-act race and the unlocked roster read/modify/write. Per master plan line 81, process-level hub locking is Slice 4's responsibility; an interim init-race half-measure was reverted because re-checking .beads can mask a partial-init failure. See federated-beads-dxh.5 comment.

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text: Autoreview round 5 (deferred, out of scope): ensure_hub is additive-only and does not prune hub entries removed from the roster. Slice 3's scope per the plan is additive reconciliation (adds_missing_repos_only); removal needs a new BdClient::repo_remove capability (bd supports 'bd repo remove') or a reset-rebuild strategy, best designed with Slice 7's roster CLI. 'fbd reset' already rebuilds from the current roster as an interim workaround. Filed follow-up bead. Accepted+fixed this round earlier: dangling-symlink reset (symlink_metadata) and hub-init detection keyed on .beads/embeddeddolt (bd-parity).

title: Slice 1: Domain types — parse recorded bd --json fixtures
description: Record real bd 1.1.0 outputs into tests/fixtures/ (ready.json, show.json array-of-one with dependencies, search.json, version.json). Red: parsing tests asserting ids, int priority, Option description tolerant of omitted keys, dependencies[0].dependency_type==blocks, schema_version==1. Green: Issue, IssueDetail, Dependency, BdVersion serde types — no deny_unknown_fields, Option/default for every omittable field. Refactor: Vec<IssueDetail>::into_single() with clear 0/N error. See plans/fbd-v1-implementation-plan.md, Slice 1, for full red/green/refactor detail.
acceptance_criteria: All four fixture parsing tests green; serde types tolerate absent optional keys and unknown future keys

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text: Autoreview (codex gpt-5.6-sol, high): 1 finding, SKIPPED. [P2] fixtures (ready/show/search.json) contain owner email bellbm@gmail.com. Rejected because: (a) team lead explicitly required verbatim real-bd fixtures; (b) the same email is already the git commit-author identity on every commit, so scrubbing only the fixtures yields ~zero marginal privacy benefit; (c) repo is local-only, no push authorized; (d) no test asserts owner/created_by values (shape-only). Flagged to team lead: if PII scrubbing is desired, both fixtures AND git author identity would need sanitizing together.

title: Slice 2: BdClient trait + BdCli subprocess client + FakeBdClient
description: Red (unit): builds_correct_argv for version/ready/export/repo_sync/repo_add/init/show/search (bd -C forms). Red (gated integration): version_and_ready_roundtrip builds a real fixture repo (init/create x3/link/export) in tempdir and asserts ready excludes the blocked issue; skips with eprintln SKIP when bd absent. Green: BdClient trait, BdCli via std::process::Command with exit-status check, stdout JSON parse, BdError{command,stderr,kind}; FakeBdClient with programmable responses. Refactor: generic run_json<T> helper, stderr captured/truncated. See plans/fbd-v1-implementation-plan.md, Slice 2, for full red/green/refactor detail.
acceptance_criteria: Argv unit tests green; integration test green with bd installed and prints SKIP without it

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text: Autoreview (codex gpt-5.6-sol, 5 rounds → clean): fixed all findings, none skipped. (1) BdCli::default() delegated to new() so it spawns 'bd' not ''. (2/3) FakeBdClient made fully error-programmable across the trait surface (with_*_err) + per-repo export failures. (4) argv built as OsString to preserve non-UTF-8 Unix paths. (5) real-bd-verified argv fixes: export '-o .beads/issues.jsonl' (relative to -C, avoids path doubling), ready/search '--limit 0' (bd caps at 100/50), search '--query=<q>' (flag-like queries stay literal).

title: Slice 0: Harness — cargo scaffold, config round-trip, git/GitHub setup
description: Stand up the project so TDD is possible: cargo init (name fbd) with deps (ratatui, crossterm, serde, serde_json, toml, clap derive, anyhow, thiserror, dirs; tempfile dev). First red test: config::tests::roundtrip_roster (save/load Config{repos} via toml in tempdir). Green: config.rs with Config/RepoEntry/load/save. Refactor: injectable Paths resolver (XDG behind it). Also: git init, .gitignore, README documenting the four verification commands, initial commit, gh repo create. See plans/fbd-v1-implementation-plan.md, Slice 0, for full red/green/refactor detail.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test runs and roundtrip_roster is green; cargo fmt --check and clippy -D warnings clean; repo exists on GitHub

comments.author: Brian Bell
comments.text: Autoreview (codex/gpt-5.6-sol, high): 1 finding, fixed, 0 skipped. [P2] config.rs save() used fs::write which truncates the roster (source of truth) before writing -> interrupted write could corrupt/empty config.toml. Fixed: save() now writes to a same-directory temp file (.config.toml.tmp.<pid>) and atomically renames over the destination. Added test save_overwrites_existing_file. Rerun autoreview: clean (patch correct 0.93).

title: fbd reset can be resurrected by an in-flight refresh's cache write
description:
autoreview (gpt-5.6-luna, high) on the snapshot-cache branch flagged: fbd reset clears snapshot_cache.json and the hub dir, but does not coordinate with a concurrently-running TUI's in-flight refresh worker. If a refresh's gather_snapshot pipeline (sync -> bd ready -> cache::save) is still running when reset fires in another terminal, its cache::save can land after reset's cache::clear and recreate a snapshot reflecting the just-discarded hub, which the next launch would then hydrate for up to cache::MAX_AGE (12h).

Root cause is deeper than the cache file itself: hub::reset() deletes the hub directory without acquiring the same advisory hub lock refresh::run holds during sync (src/hub.rs reset() vs src/refresh.rs HubLock), so reset already isn't coordinated with an in-flight refresh at the hub level. A full fix likely needs a reset generation/token (or lock-widening) checked before any refresh worker's cache::save, not just a cache-local guard.

Deferred out of the snapshot-cache feature (federated-beads current work) as a narrow, multi-process edge case: requires 'fbd reset' racing a live 'fbd' TUI's refresh within the same few seconds. Consciously not fixed there to avoid a broad hub/refresh locking change beyond that feature's scope.
acceptance_criteria: Concurrently running 'fbd reset' in one terminal while another terminal's fbd TUI has an in-flight refresh cannot resurrect a stale snapshot_cache.json reflecting pre-reset hub state on the next launch.

title: v2: cache the refresh PrefixMap so cross-repo search reuses it instead of rebuilding per query
description: Slice 11's search worker rebuilds the prefix map from the roster on every query via refresh::attribution_map (one bd issue_prefix subprocess per repo, ~1s for 5 repos). The map only changes when the roster or a repo's prefix changes. v2: cache the last successful refresh's PrefixMap (e.g. runtime-side, refreshed on each RefreshCompleted) and hand a clone to the search worker, eliminating the per-search prefix reads. Keep the pure App free of PrefixMap.

title: Detail pane: tag bd show requests with a generation to drop stale same-id responses
description: Autoreview (slice 10) finding: Msg::DetailReady matches only on issue id. Opening X, pressing Esc, then re-opening X before the first bd show returns spawns two workers that both return id=X; whichever lands last wins, so an older (possibly errored) response can overwrite the newer pane for the same issue. Low severity (same issue data), beyond slice 10's plan scope (which dropped stale responses only for a *different* id). Fix: tag Effect::FetchDetail/Msg::DetailReady with a monotonic generation set on OpenDetail and require both generation and id to match the active request in reduce.
close_reason: Fixed in slice 10 (autoreview round 2): Msg::DetailReady now matches by monotonic request token, so reopening the same issue drops the earlier worker's late response. Tests same_id_reopen_drops_earlier_response, stale_detail_response_is_dropped.

title: v2: ready-list navigation should follow grouped display order
description: Slice 9 buckets rows by repo in the view, but the App's flat order (selection space) is global priority order (slice 5). So j/k walk priority order while the eye sees repo sections, and the highlight can jump between sections when a repo's rows are non-contiguous in priority. Making navigation follow grouped display order needs the App/snapshot row order itself to be grouped-then-sorted, which was out of scope for a render-only slice. See plans/slices/slice-9.md decision 2.

title: Serialize roster read-modify-write (fbd repos add/remove/discover)
description: Autoreview (slice 7) flagged a lost-update race: two concurrent `fbd repos add/remove/discover --add` both load config.toml, mutate, and Config::save (atomic rename) — the last writer silently discards the other's change. No corruption (save is atomic), only a lost entry. Consciously deferred in slice 7 as speculative for a human-driven personal roster CLI, consistent with the slice-6 deferred hub-lock call (federated-beads-dxh.16). Fix if it ever bites: hold an exclusive lock (or compare-and-retry) across load+mutate+save of the roster.

title: Harden cross-process hub locking: cover ensure_hub reconciliation and reset
description: Autoreview (slice 6) flagged that the refresh advisory lock (<hub>/.fbd.lock) is scoped to 'repo sync' only. ensure_hub's init/repo_add reconciliation runs before the lock is taken, and 'fbd reset' deletes the hub with no lock at all, so two concurrent fbd processes (or reset-during-refresh) can race on the shared hub. The hub is disposable derived data and Slices 3/4 deliberately scoped the lock to sync, so this was deferred out of slice 6. Design a coherent locking protocol that (a) guards ensure_hub reconciliation, (b) makes reset refuse/defer while a refresh holds the lock, without self-deadlocking a single process (fs2 flock is per-fd). Note: nesting HubLock inside run_snapshot around ensure_hub would conflict with refresh::run's own acquisition.

title: snapshot: parse updated_at before sorting if bd emits fractional/offset timestamps
description: Slice 5's snapshot sort compares updated_at as raw RFC3339 strings, which is chronological only because bd 1.1.0 emits homogeneous whole-second UTC-Z timestamps within a single 'bd ready' call (assumption documented at the comparator in src/snapshot.rs). If a future bd emits fractional seconds or non-Z offsets, lexical order could diverge from chronological. If/when that happens, parse timestamps (add chrono/time as a deliberate project-wide dep) before comparing, preserving None-last behavior, and add coverage for fractional seconds/offsets. Raised by autoreview on slice 5; consciously deferred.

title: ensure_hub: prune stale hub entries dropped from the roster
description: Slice 3 ensure_hub is additive-only: it repo_adds missing roster entries but never removes hub config.yaml entries that are no longer in config.toml, so a removed repo keeps getting synced/shown. bd supports 'bd repo remove <path>' but BdClient has no repo_remove method. Options: (a) add BdClient::repo_remove + prune non-roster entries in ensure_hub; (b) reset+rebuild when the sets differ. Design alongside Slice 7 (fbd repos remove). Workaround today: 'fbd reset' rebuilds the hub from the current roster (hub is disposable derived data). Out of scope for Slice 3 per plan (adds_missing_repos_only).

title: Revisit BdClient::repo_list — bd repo list --json emits non-JSON text
description: Slice 3 found that 'bd repo list --json' ignores --json and prints human-readable text, so BdClient::repo_list (run_json) returns a Parse error against real bd 1.1.0. ensure_hub instead reads <hub>/.beads/config.yaml repos.additional. repo_list is effectively dead trait surface — decide whether to remove it or reshape it to parse the text output. Also revisit serde_yaml (deprecated-but-stable) dependency.
